mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, the discount rate on equities declines and risk appetite improves, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because falling policy rates then read as easing rather than inflation panic.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window while CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or lower, or if the conditional Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and slower final demand, which should pressure equities lower over the next month. The effect should appear after the unemployment rate crosses up rather than on small fluctuations around trend.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if upward 3-month UNRATE crossings are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in HOUST indicates stronger residential construction activity and broader cyclical demand, which should support equity returns over the next quarter. This mechanism fails when housing gains occur alongside a tightening policy stance, because higher rates then overwhelm demand strength.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are negative in more than half of housing-upturn triggers, or if the signal disappears when FEDFUNDS is above 5% and HOUST is still rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates improving real activity without immediate inflation pressure, which should translate into higher equities over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if industrial production gains are accompanied by rising inflation and the stock market sells off instead.
failure_condition: Reject if the conditional average 20-day SPX return after positive INDPRO momentum and non-rising CPIAUCSL is not above zero, or if the spread versus all other days is insignificant in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals softening sales and labor income, which should reduce earnings expectations and push equities lower over the next week. The effect is a short-horizon demand shock rather than a long-run trend.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls and PAYEMS falls over the same 1-month window
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly declines in RSAFS and PAYEMS are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal remains null after excluding revision-prone months.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS rate above the recent neutral range tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating further, because then the policy stance dominates rather than an inflation repricing.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not have below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate for negative returns is under 55% across the tightening regime.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend reflects weakening payroll creation, which should precede lower corporate revenue growth and lower equity returns over the next quarter. The signal should work best when unemployment is also rising, confirming that labor softness is broad-based rather than a measurement artifact.
failure_condition: Reject if declining payroll momentum combined with rising UNRATE does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal flips positive during sustained labor deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: A weakening GDP growth trend in GDPC1 combined with flat or falling RSAFS indicates broad demand deceleration, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism should fail when GDP is weak but sales remain resilient, because then the macro slowdown is not yet broad enough to hit earnings.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 momentum paired with flat or falling RSAFS is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not significantly below baseline.
